question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConstructionCombine()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a crafted UnsignedTransaction hex blob in which the serialized protobuf body bytes differ from what was originally produced by ConstructionPayloads(), causing getFrozenTransactionBodyBytes() to extract attacker-controlled BodyBytes that are then signed and committed to Hashgraph history as a legitimate transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConstructionCombine()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a UnsignedTransaction whose inner SignedTransaction.BodyBytes encodes a different payer account than the one agreed upon in ConstructionPayloads(), so that ed25519.Verify() passes against the attacker-supplied frozenBodyBytes and the wrong payer's history is permanently altered on Hashgraph?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConstructionCombine()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a valid signature over attacker-chosen BodyBytes by first calling ConstructionPayloads() with legitimate operations, then replacing the UnsignedTransaction bytes before calling ConstructionCombine(), bypassing the lack of cross-request binding and injecting a falsified transfer into Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConstructionCombine()] Can an unprivileged external user replay a previously observed signed transaction hex string through ConstructionCombine() followed by ConstructionSubmit() to re-execute a historical Hashgraph transfer, causing duplicate debits and corrupting the ledger history of the victim account?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConstructionCombine()] Can an unprivileged external user attach multiple signatures from different public keys to a single UnsignedTransaction via the loop over request.Signatures, where each signature is verified independently against the same frozenBodyBytes, allowing an attacker to add spurious signers to a transaction and manipulate the multi-sig authorization record stored in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFrozenTransactionBodyBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a hex-encoded transaction whose prototext representation, when parsed by prototext.Unmarshal() into services.SignedTransaction{}, yields BodyBytes that differ from the actual serialized transaction body, causing the signature verification in ConstructionCombine() to validate a signature over attacker-controlled content and permanently record a tampered transaction on Hashgraph?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getFrozenTransactionBodyBytes()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a transaction where hiero.TransactionString() returns a prototext that contains extra or overriding BodyBytes fields, exploiting protobuf/prototext field ordering or duplicate field behavior so that prototext.Unmarshal() picks up attacker-injected BodyBytes, leading to a signature being verified against falsified transaction content that gets submitted to Hashgraph?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: unmarshallTransactionFromHexString()] Can an unprivileged external user provide a hex string that decodes to a valid protobuf-serialized transaction of a supported type (AccountCreateTransaction or TransferTransaction) but with internal fields set to attacker-chosen values (e.g., altered node account ID, memo, or transfer amounts), bypassing isSupportedTransactionType() which only checks the Go type and not field-level integrity, allowing the tampered transaction to be submitted to Hashgraph?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: unmarshallTransactionFromHexString()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a hex-encoded transaction with a valid outer structure but with a TransactionID referencing an arbitrary payer account and timestamp, so that after unmarshalling the transaction is accepted by isSupportedTransactionType() and submitted, permanently recording a falsified payer identity in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConstructionSubmit()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a fully-formed signed transaction hex string directly to ConstructionSubmit() without going through ConstructionPayloads() or ConstructionCombine(), bypassing all server-side construction checks, and cause an arbitrary transaction to be executed and permanently recorded in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConstructionSubmit()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a manipulated node account ID (via transaction.GetNodeAccountIDs()[0]) to route the transaction to a specific consensus node, potentially exploiting node-specific behavior to influence how the transaction is ordered and recorded in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConstructionSubmit()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a signed transaction where hiero.TransactionGetTransactionHash() returns a hash that does not correspond to the actual transaction body, causing the logged and returned hash to diverge from the on-chain Hashgraph record and enabling history falsification through hash mismatch?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConstructionSubmit()] Can an unprivileged external user repeatedly submit the same signed transaction hex to ConstructionSubmit() in rapid succession, exploiting the absence of server-side deduplication or idempotency checks, to attempt replay of a transaction and corrupt Hashgraph history with duplicate entries?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionSetTransactionId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a metadataKeyValidStartNanos value of 0 in the ConstructionPayloads() metadata, causing transactionSetTransactionId() to call hiero.TransactionIDGenerate(payer) with a server-generated timestamp, and then manipulate the resulting transaction ID to collide with or shadow an existing Hashgraph transaction, corrupting the historical record?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionSetTransactionId()] Can an unprivileged external user provide an arbitrarily large or far-future validStartNanos value via metadataKeyValidStartNanos, causing hiero.NewTransactionIDWithValidStart() to create a transaction ID with a timestamp far in the future, which when submitted could displace or shadow legitimate future transactions in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionSetTransactionId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a validStartNanos value that matches the timestamp of a previously executed transaction for the same payer account, causing a transaction ID collision that could confuse Hashgraph deduplication logic and potentially allow re-execution or shadowing of historical transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTransactionTimestampProperty()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a metadataKeyValidUntilNanos value that is exactly maxValidDurationNanos+1, causing validStartNanos to be computed as 1 (barely positive), effectively setting the transaction valid start to the Unix epoch (January 1, 1970), which could collide with genesis-era Hashgraph transactions and corrupt historical records?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTransactionTimestampProperty()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a metadataKeyValidUntilNanos value as a string encoding a very large int64 (near MaxInt64), causing the arithmetic validUntil - maxValidDurationNanos to overflow and produce a negative or zero validStartNanos that bypasses the validStartNanos <= 0 check due to integer overflow, resulting in a transaction with an invalid timestamp being submitted to Hashgraph?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTransactionTimestampProperty()] Can an unprivileged external user omit metadataKeyValidUntilNanos and instead supply metadataKeyValidDurationSeconds=0 and metadataKeyValidStartNanos=0, causing isValidTransactionValidDuration() to accept 0 as valid and transactionSetTransactionId() to use server-generated timing, then manipulate the resulting transaction to conflict with existing Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getTransactionTimestampProperty()] Can an unprivileged external user supply metadataKeyValidDurationSeconds=180 (maxValidDurationSeconds) and a metadataKeyValidStartNanos set to a past timestamp, causing the transaction window to span a historical period and potentially overlap with already-executed transactions in Hashgraph, enabling replay or confusion attacks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: transactionSetValidDuration()] Can an unprivileged external user supply validDurationSeconds=0 to trigger the default of 180 seconds in transactionSetValidDuration(), and then combine this with a carefully chosen validStartNanos to maximize the transaction's validity window, increasing the chance of a replay attack succeeding against Hashgraph?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isValidTransactionValidDuration()] Can an unprivileged external user supply validDurationSeconds=0 which passes the isValidTransactionValidDuration() check (since 0 >= 0 && 0 <= 180), and then have transactionSetValidDuration() silently replace it with 180 seconds, creating a discrepancy between the user-specified duration and the actual transaction duration submitted to Hashgraph, potentially enabling timing-based manipulation of transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: ConstructionPayloads()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a crafted metadataKeyAccountMap string into the ConstructionPayloads() metadata to manipulate getSdkPayerAccountId() into resolving an alias to an attacker-controlled account ID, causing the transaction to be constructed with a falsified payer that gets permanently recorded in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSdkPayerAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a metadataKeyAccountMap string with a malformed mapping (e.g., missing colon separator or extra colons) so that mapping[1] in strings.Split(aliasMap, ':') returns an unexpected segment, causing hiero.AccountIDFromString() to parse an attacker-chosen account ID as the payer, permanently recording a falsified payer in Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: getSdkPayerAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply an accountMapMetadata string where the alias prefix matching via strings.HasPrefix() is exploited by crafting an alias that is a prefix of a different legitimate alias (e.g., alias '0.0.1' matching '0.0.10:0.0.999'), causing the wrong account ID to be used as the payer and corrupting the transaction's payer record in Hashgraph history?
